title: Why Thresholds Differ by Disease
script_summary: Exploring how different diseases require varying levels of immunity to control their spread.

segments.voiceover: Did you know that more contagious diseases need higher immunity coverage?
Herd immunity thresholds aren't one-size-fits-all. They vary based on the disease.
For example, measles needs about 95% immunity to halt outbreaks. Why so high?
More transmissible viruses like COVID-19 require similar thresholds. The data is clear.
Understanding these thresholds helps us prepare and protect ourselves better.
Stay informed about vaccines and herd immunity. Follow for more insights.
